its sorta complicated to explain without spoiling something that is revealed towards the end of arc 1, but its not wrong that the dissolution is related to the spiritual and physical realms becoming thin, and some domains __are__ a byproduct of dissolution
but domains can just as easily form naturally without dissolution, either by personal means (such as daffodil) or artificially (artificial domains)
for now just think of domains as spaces in-between realities

there's a few canonical ancient languages, so i'm not sure which particular one they would be referring to for that subject

latin - but it's not actually that ancient in the morimens lore since it's implied the roman empire's fall was *relatively* recent. a lot of educated people know bits and pieces of it as well

the language spoken by thais and the aram people - after the great flood, they're the only ones still using it as their mother tongue. the keeper of secrets does know this language as well. based on the location of chapter 7 and it being referred to as a very ancient language, it's almost certainly a stand-in for akkadian, but the exact language inspiration isn't definitively clarified

lemurian - already mentioned by niko, though it's probably informative to note that lemurian is considered to be even more ancient than akkadian etc. since it's pre-holocene

juliette's native language? - it was mentioned on a few occasions that some of the ancient texts of the lightbearers are written in their own language that is unrelated to lemurian, but they never really 100% clarified which people it was supposed to belong to (if any)
i find the languages referenced in the game to be kind of interesting so i kept a note of them in my head
By *relatively*, do you mean that compared to other events of the Morimens world the fall of the romam empire was recent or did the fall happen way later compared to our world?

it's pretty strongly-evidenced in the lore but also frequently contradicted that the fall of rome was only maybe 150-250 years before the present period. it actually seems like the roman empire was less successful in morimens' world history than it was in our earth's world history as well, it's just that a lot less time passed in morimens' world history between that event and the "victorian period" in our world history
londinium has a pseudo-victorian aesthetic rather than actually being genuinely victorian, basically

technically unless this has gotten disposed of in the more recent lore, morimens' world history had already had an industrial revolution prior to the "great flood" that was part of the bronze age collapse https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Late_Bronze_Age_collapse

embeds.title: Late Bronze Age collapse
embeds.description: The Late Bronze Age collapse was a period of societal collapse in the Mediterranean basin during the late 13th to early 12th century BC. It is thought to have affected much of the Eastern Mediterranean and Near East, in particular Egypt, Anatolia, the Aegean, eastern Libya, and the Balkans. The coll
